description: Structured output schema for the brainstorm agent. Every field is required — the agent must not proceed without answering all of them.

app_name.description: Short, memorable app name (2-3 words max)

dir_slug.description: Kebab-case directory slug derived from what the app does — not the brand name. 1-3 words. Examples: daily-ritual, sleep-tracker, water-log, habit-streak. No brand names, no clever wordplay.

problem_statement.description: The specific problem this app solves, written as a user story: 'When I [situation], I struggle to [problem] because [root cause]'

target_user.description: Concrete description of the primary user. Not a demographic — a person with a specific context and need.

why_existing_solutions_fail.description: What does the user try today, and why is it not good enough? Must name at least one real alternative.

hero_feature.description: The single feature that defines this app's value. One sentence.

out_of_scope.description: Features explicitly excluded from the MVP. These are guardrails for the builder agent.

mvp_screens.description: Exhaustive list of screens in the MVP. The builder agent will build exactly these — no more. No paywall screen — all apps are free.

success_criteria.description: Testable acceptance criteria. Each must be verifiable by the QA agent without human judgment.
